claim: DHS and NIST published 2021 guidance for preparing for post-quantum cryptography by inventorying and prioritizing cryptographic systems and data.

dhs_memorandum_page.title: Memorandum on Preparing for Post-Quantum Cryptography

verified_points: DHS says it released a roadmap with NIST to help organizations protect data and systems and reduce risks related to quantum computing technology.
DHS says organizations should consider inventorying current cryptographic systems, the data being protected, and prioritizing systems for transition.
DHS says preparation includes identifying, prioritizing, and protecting potentially vulnerable data, algorithms, protocols, and systems.

DHS internal transition work is expected to result in an inventory of DHS cryptographic systems and data types, broader enterprise risk understanding, and plans for transition to post-quantum cryptography.
The DHS memorandum states that previous transitions to new cryptographic standards have taken between 5 and 15 years given breadth of use.

roadmap_steps.label: standards_engagement
roadmap_steps.summary: Increase CIO engagement with standards developing organizations for algorithm and protocol changes.

roadmap_steps.label: critical_data_inventory
roadmap_steps.summary: Inventory the most sensitive and critical datasets that must remain secure for an extended time.

roadmap_steps.label: cryptographic_system_inventory
roadmap_steps.summary: Inventory systems using cryptographic technologies for any function.

roadmap_steps.label: standards_update_identification
roadmap_steps.summary: Identify acquisition, cybersecurity, and data security standards that will require post-quantum updates.

roadmap_steps.label: public_key_usage_identification
roadmap_steps.summary: Identify where and why public-key cryptography is used and mark those systems as quantum vulnerable.

roadmap_steps.label: risk_based_prioritization
roadmap_steps.summary: Prioritize quantum-vulnerable systems using organizational mission, data sensitivity, sharing relationships, critical infrastructure support, and required protection lifetime.

roadmap_steps.label: transition_planning
roadmap_steps.summary: Use inventory and prioritization information to develop transition plans once post-quantum cryptographic standards are available.

scope_limits: The public DHS roadmap is preparation guidance, not a claim that all organizations are legally required by this document to migrate by a fixed date.
The DHS memorandum's component inventory and transition-plan submission requirements are internal DHS preparedness guidance.
DHS cautions against purchasing or implementing market PQC products before NIST-approved standards, because premature adoption can create a harder and more costly transition.
Use this source to support discovery, inventory, prioritization, and transition planning; do not use it as proof that our implementation fully satisfies all DHS or NIST guidance.
presentation_guidance: Use DHS 2021 as the official roadmap source for the flow: identify critical data, inventory cryptography, mark quantum-vulnerable public-key systems, prioritize, and plan transition.
If citing a migration-duration figure from DHS, say previous cryptographic standard transitions have taken 5 to 15 years, not that DHS measured average PQC migration as 5 to 10 years.
Connect the source to our project as policy alignment: our software makes the discovery and inventory portion of this roadmap executable in a demo environment.
